Access card issue — only one card is usually provided, but you’ll need two (one for lift access and one for room power). If one person stays in the room, the other may get stuck without lift access. Sentence: I found this hotel and booked it literally 30 minutes before I checked in, and the staff were still very accommodating although a little unapproachable. My room was big and pretty clean, and while the bed is not 5-star style it’s still quite comfortable. And even though I faced both a construction site and the main road it wasn’t very noisy. The hotel itself could do with some light maintenance tho - the corridors are a bit dirty and the ground floor lobby needs a spruce to make it look more inviting. But my biggest peeve was the elevator. The middle elevator (inevitably the one I got every time) wouldn’t access my floor with my keycard. I had to stop at reception and ask them to tap their pass for me. Not a dealbreaker coz they’re 24hr but annoying enough. When I pressed them on this they said they had a limited number of keys that allowed floor access and mine wasn’t one of them - which is patently stupid. I hope they fix this as the rest of the stay was nice otherwise.

The facilities are quite new, the check-in is done by taking a number and queueing, and the efficiency of the elevators is decent. The rooms are also modernly decorated and the view is great. The only dissatisfaction is that I lost some electronic products (which I left on the table before going out, and they were missing when I returned). Since there are no cameras inside, I didn’t want to make it too troublesome, but guests need to take care of their valuables during their stay.
